title: RPL and routing code uses raw [u8; 16] where Ipv6Addr newtype should be used
description:
lichen-ipv6 defines Addr([u8; 16]) and lichen-core re-exports it as Ipv6Addr. However, routing code uses raw arrays:

lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs:
- RoutingTable keyed by [u8; 16]
- SourceRoutingHeader stores Vec<[u8; 16]>
- RouteEntry has target: [u8; 16]

lichen-node/src/routing.rs:
- Neighbor { addr: [u8; 16], ... }
- NeighborTable uses [u8; 16] keys
- Router stores HashMap<[u8; 16], ...>

lichen-gateway/src/gateway.rs:
- mesh_to_upstream returns Vec<u8> containing raw IPv6

What goes wrong: Without the newtype, callers can accidentally pass NodeId bytes (8 bytes) where IPv6 addresses (16 bytes) are expected, or mix up address types. The Ipv6Addr methods (is_link_local, is_multicast, iid) are unavailable on raw arrays.
notes: Preparatory work completed: lichen-ipv6::Addr exists and is re-exported as lichen_core::addr::Ipv6Addr. Remaining work: replace ~30 [u8;16] usages with Ipv6Addr in lichen-rpl/routing.rs and lichen-node/routing.rs, update tests. The Addr type has Copy+Hash so it works as HashMap key.
